footer.tagline: Open-source beekeeping for a healthier planet.
footer.copyright: © 2025 HivePulse. Released under the MIT License.

hero.badge: Open Source · Community Driven · Free to Use
hero.subtitle: HivePulse turns every beekeeper into a citizen scientist. Log inspections, track hive health, and contribute to global data that helps researchers understand and protect honey bee populations worldwide.

stats.title: Our Community, Right Now
stats.sub: Real-time numbers from our global network of beekeepers

feat.title: Everything a beekeeper needs
feat.sub: From first-time hobbyist to commercial operation, HivePulse grows with you — and your data grows with the planet.

qr.title: QR Scan & Go
qr.desc: Stick a QR code on each hive. Scan it on arrival and jump straight into your inspection — no searching, no typos, no forgotten hive names.

track.title: Smart Tracking
track.desc: Track brood frames, varroa counts, queen status, honey stores, and hive mood over time. Spot trends before they become problems.

global.title: Global Data Pool
global.desc: Your anonymized data contributes to a global picture of hive health — shared with researchers studying colony collapse and disease spread.

trends.title: Trend Analysis
trends.desc: Visualize varroa counts across seasons, compare hive health across your apiary, and follow your colony's development in beautiful charts.

batch.title: QR Batch Generation
batch.desc: Generate batches of up to 50 QR codes and download a print-ready PDF — perfect for labelling a new apiary or replacing lost tags.

privacy.title: Your Data, Private
privacy.desc: Precise locations and personal notes stay private. Only anonymized aggregates are shared — you control what's visible on the public map.

mission.title: One third of everything you eat depends on bees
mission.p1: Honey bees and wild pollinators fertilize roughly 35 % of the world's food supply — fruits, vegetables, nuts, and oils. But populations have been declining for decades, and science is still scrambling to understand exactly why.
mission.li1: Colony Collapse Disorder affects millions of hives every year
mission.li2: Varroa mite is the #1 threat to managed bee colonies globally
mission.li3: Pesticides, habitat loss, and climate change compound the pressure
mission.li4: Real-time beekeeper data is essential for early-warning systems
mission.callout: When you log an inspection in HivePulse, you're not just managing your hive — you're adding a data point to a global early-warning network for bee health.

hex1.label: of flowering crops depend on animal pollination

hex2.label: of insect pollinator species face extinction risk

hex3.label: annual value of global pollination services

comm.title: Why community data changes everything
comm.sub: A single beekeeper sees one apiary. Together, we see the world — and we can do something about it.

disease.title: Disease Mapping
disease.desc: Identify geographic clusters of varroa infestation or disease outbreaks before they spread, enabling targeted beekeeper intervention across regions.

seasonal.title: Seasonal Trends
seasonal.desc: Cross-reference hive health with weather patterns, bloom calendars, and local land-use to pinpoint what's driving colony losses in each area.

research.title: Research Partnership
research.desc: Our anonymized dataset is shared with university researchers and conservation NGOs, contributing to peer-reviewed science on pollinator health.

policy.title: Policy Change
policy.desc: Hard data from thousands of apiaries gives beekeepers a powerful evidence-based voice in conversations about pesticide regulation and habitat protection.

road.title: On the Roadmap
road.sub: We're honest about what's shipped and what's planned. Here's where we're heading.

remind.title: Inspection Reminders
remind.desc: Push notifications when a hive is overdue for inspection.

export.title: Data Export (CSV / JSON)
export.desc: Download your complete inspection history as CSV or JSON — now live in the web dashboard.

fields.title: Custom Fields in Forms
fields.desc: Define your own inspection fields and have them appear in every inspection form — now live in the web dashboard.

heat.title: Regional Heatmaps
heat.desc: Varroa risk overlays on the public live map, colour-coded by mite pressure — now live.

support.title: Keep the hives buzzing
support.sub: HivePulse is free to use. A small contribution keeps the servers running, funds new features, and supports our research partnerships. No beekeeper should be priced out.

sup.f5: Your name in the app credits

donate.note: Every contribution, no matter the size, helps keep HivePulse independent, ad-free, and free for beekeepers who need it most.

dl.title: Download HivePulse — it's free
dl.sub: Available on iOS and Android. Scan your first hive in under two minutes.

hero.title: See every hive we're protecting
hero.subtitle: Explore apiaries registered by beekeepers around the world — updated in real time.

preview.title: See our community on the map
preview.sub: Explore apiaries registered by beekeepers around the world — updated in real time.

cluster.title: Cluster Markers
cluster.desc: Nearby apiaries group into clusters that expand as you zoom in, keeping the map readable at any scale.

heatmap.title: Heatmap Toggle
heatmap.desc: Switch on the varroa heatmap to see regional mite pressure at a glance, colour-coded from low to high.

fuzzed.title: Fuzzed Locations
fuzzed.desc: Exact hive coordinates are never shown publicly — pins are randomly offset to protect beekeepers' privacy.

news.title: News & Announcements
news.sub: New features, research partnerships, conservation milestones, and everything happening in the HivePulse community.

contrib.title: Help Build HivePulse
contrib.sub: HivePulse is an open-source project built by and for the beekeeping community. Every contribution — whether code, documentation, translation, or feedback — makes a difference.

ways.title: Ways to Contribute
ways.sub: You don't need to be a programmer to help. There's a meaningful role for everyone who cares about bees.

bug.title: Report Bugs
bug.desc: Found something that doesn't work? Open a GitHub issue with device info, OS version, and steps to reproduce.

code.title: Submit Code
code.desc: Fork the repo, create a branch, open a PR. Backend (Python), iOS (Swift), Android (Kotlin) — all welcome.

docs.title: Improve Documentation
docs.desc: Help improve the in-app help text, the API contract, architecture notes, or this website.

trans.title: Translate the App
trans.desc: Bilingual? Help add or improve a translation. iOS uses .strings files, Android uses XML resources — no programming required.

test.title: Test New Features
test.desc: Join the beta program and give feedback before features ship. Your real-world perspective beats any automated test.

spread.title: Spread the Word
spread.desc: Tell your beekeeping club or online community about HivePulse. Every new beekeeper strengthens the global dataset.

github.title: 🐝 The Hive is on GitHub
github.desc: HivePulse is fully open source under the MIT License. Read the code, open issues, fork it, and make it better.

fr.title: Request a Feature or Report a Bug
fr.sub: Fill in the form and we'll open a pre-filled GitHub issue for you — one click to submit. No account needed to preview; GitHub login required to post.

ph.title: Short, descriptive title
ph.desc: Describe the feature or bug in detail. What should happen? What does happen?
ph.usecase: Why do you need this? How would you use it?

fr.note: You'll be redirected to GitHub to review and submit the issue.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: HivePulse is an open-source beekeeping app. This policy explains what data we collect, why we collect it, and the choices you have — including how you control whether your apiary appears on the public map.
privacy.s1body: When you register: your email address, chosen display name, and preferred language. When you use the app: apiary names and GPS coordinates (if you enter them), hive details, and inspection records you log. We do not collect device identifiers, advertising IDs, or precise location data without you explicitly entering coordinates in the app.
privacy.s2body: The public map shows anonymised apiary pins to help visualise global beekeeping activity. Participation is strictly opt-in. By default every apiary you create is private — it never appears on the map and is never exposed via the public API. You can enable 'Show on public map' per apiary in the app settings at any time and disable it again just as easily. When enabled, the pin shows your apiary name, GPS coordinates, and hive count. No user identity or inspection detail is ever shared publicly.
privacy.s3body: Your data is used solely to run the app (authentication, storing and displaying your records) and, only if you opt in, to show your apiary pin on the public map and contribute aggregate counts to the global statistics (total apiaries, hives, inspections). We do not sell, trade, or rent personal data. We do not use it for advertising.
privacy.s4body: For EU/EEA users, we process your data on the basis of contract performance (Art. 6(1)(b) GDPR) for the core app features, and on the basis of your explicit consent (Art. 6(1)(a) GDPR) for public map participation. You can withdraw consent at any time by toggling the 'Show on public map' switch off.
privacy.s5body: Your account data is kept for as long as your account is active. You can request full deletion by contacting us at the address below — we will delete your account and all associated records within 30 days. Anonymised aggregate statistics (total counts) that have already been served publicly cannot be retracted.
privacy.s6body: Under GDPR you have the right to access, rectify, erase, and port your personal data, and to object to processing. To exercise these rights, contact us at privacy@HivePulse.app. If you believe your rights have not been respected, you may lodge a complaint with your national data protection authority.
privacy.s7body: Passwords are hashed with bcrypt and never stored in plain text. API access requires a signed JWT token. Data in transit is encrypted via TLS. We review security practices regularly.
privacy.s8body: Questions about this policy or your data? Email us at privacy@HivePulse.app.

forgotPassword.title: Reset your password
forgotPassword.desc: Enter your email address and we'll send you a reset link.
forgotPassword.sent: If that email is registered, you'll receive a reset link shortly. Check your inbox.

resetPassword.title: Set a new password
resetPassword.success: Password changed. Redirecting to log in…
resetPassword.tooShort: Password must be at least 8 characters.
resetPassword.invalidLink: This reset link is invalid. Please request a new one.
resetPassword.errorGeneric: Reset failed. The link may have expired.

profile.title: Profile
profile.name: Name
profile.errorGeneric: Something went wrong. Please try again.

profile.deleteConfirm: Are you sure? This will permanently delete all your data.

users.title: User Management
users.name: Name

users.confirmRevoke: Revoke all sessions for this user?

hive.deleteConfirmText: This will permanently delete the hive and all its inspections.

fieldDefs.title: Custom Fields
fieldDefs.subtitle: Extend your hive and inspection forms with custom fields.
fieldDefs.name: Field name
fieldDefs.deleteConfirmText: Delete this field? Data using this field will be lost.
fieldDefs.apiarySubtitle: These fields only apply to hives and inspections in this apiary.

traps.myEmpty: You have no registered traps yet.
traps.name: Name

members.title: Community Insights
members.sub: Supporters unlock deeper statistics, global trends, and research-grade data that help you become a better beekeeper — and help science understand our bees.

snap.title: Global Community Snapshot
snap.sub: These numbers are available to everyone. Supporters see much more.

teaser.title: Advanced Global Statistics
teaser.subUnlocked: Live community data — open your dashboard
teaser.subMember: Live community data — yours to explore

gate.title: Unlock the full picture
gate.unlockedNote: Live global statistics will appear here when the web dashboard launches. All features are available now in the app.
gate.desc: Supporters see regional varroa trends, seasonal hive health curves, queen replacement rates, and cross-referenced weather & bloom data — all in the HivePulse app.
gate.note: Upgrade to Supporter (€2.99 / month) inside the app after signing up.
gate.loginTitle: Log in to see live statistics
gate.loginDesc: Sign in with your HivePulse account to view live global community data.

benefits.title: What Supporters Get
benefits.sub: Every euro goes directly into keeping HivePulse independent and funding research partnerships.

b1.title: Global Health Dashboard
b1.desc: Real-time varroa index, mood distribution, and inspection frequency across all regions.

b2.title: Seasonal Trend Charts
b2.desc: How does hive health in your region compare to last year? And to beekeepers 500 km away?

b3.title: Full Data Export
b3.desc: Download your complete inspection history as CSV or JSON for your own analysis.

b4.title: Regional Heatmaps
b4.desc: Disease cluster maps with varroa risk, colony mood, swarm pressure, and brood health overlays — visible on the community dashboard.

b5.title: Research Data Portal
b5.desc: Access the anonymized dataset used in our university research partnerships.

b6.title: Early Access + Credits
b6.desc: Try new features first and get your name listed in the app. The hive remembers its builders.

coming.title: Web Dashboard for Members
coming.desc: The full web-based members dashboard is live. Explore global hive health data, mood trends, and community apiaries directly in your browser.

helpIndex.heroDesc: Step-by-step guides for every HivePulse feature — what each one does, how to use it, and what the data means for your bees.

getting-started.title: Getting started with HivePulse
getting-started.desc: An overview of HivePulse — what it does, who it is for, and how the web app, iOS app, and Android app work together.

apiaries.title: Apiaries
apiaries.desc: Create and manage apiaries — named locations that group your hives. Set a GPS location to appear on the community map.

hives.title: Hives
hives.desc: Add hives to an apiary, choose a hive type (Langstroth, Dadant, Top Bar, Warré), and link a QR code for fast field access.

qr-codes.title: QR Codes
qr-codes.desc: Generate printable QR code batches, attach them to hive boxes, and scan them to open the correct hive instantly.

custom-fields.title: Custom inspection fields
custom-fields.desc: Extend the built-in inspection form with your own fields — text, numbers, checkboxes, dropdowns — scoped to your account or a specific apiary.

inspections.title: Logging an inspection
inspections.desc: Record every visit to a hive: varroa count, colony mood, queen sighting, brood frames, honey frames, weight, treatments, and more.

data-export.title: Exporting your data
data-export.desc: Download all inspection records for an apiary as JSON or CSV to share with your veterinarian, researcher, or national beekeeping authority.

hive-stats.title: Hive statistics
hive-stats.desc: Understand your hive health over time with varroa trend charts, mood distribution, queen-seen rate, brood frame counts, and inspection intervals.

community-stats.title: Community stats (Members)
community-stats.desc: Compare your apiary against the HivePulse community — average varroa counts, good-mood rates, brood frame counts, and inspection intervals across all public apiaries.

hornet-tracker.title: Hornet Tracker
hornet-tracker.desc: Report Asian hornet catches and nest sightings, vote on community photo submissions, and view the live nest map.

hornet-traps.title: Hornet Traps
hornet-traps.desc: Register a named physical trap, log daily catch counts using its 8-character access code, and search for traps near your location.

reminders.title: Inspection reminders
reminders.desc: Set a reminder interval and season window so HivePulse can notify you when a hive is overdue for an inspection.

account.title: Account & profile
account.desc: Update your display name and language, change your password, and permanently delete your account and all associated data.

hornets.title: Asian Hornet Tracker
hornets.subtitle: Help protect bees by tracking Vespa velutina — the Asian hornet. Report catches and nests, identify sightings, and view the live map. No account needed.

info.problemText: Vespa velutina (Asian hornet) is an invasive predator that attacks honey bee colonies, weakening hives and threatening food security. Early detection is critical.
info.whyCatchText: Trapping queens in spring prevents new colonies from forming. Each caught queen can prevent thousands of workers. Community trapping makes a real difference.
info.reportNestText: If you spot a nest, do not disturb it. Report the location here so authorities can arrange safe removal. Your report saves bees.

map.title: Nest Map
map.subtitle: All reported nests — colour-coded by current status.
map.hint: View all reported nests on the live map
map.noNests: No nests reported yet. Be the first!

report.title: Report
report.catchHint: Log how many hornets you caught
report.name: Your name (optional)
report.uploadError: Photo upload failed. Please try again.
report.locationRequired: Latitude and longitude are required for nest reports.
report.success: Thank you! Your report has been submitted.

community.title: Community ID
community.subtitle: Help identify Asian hornets from photos. Vote yes if you recognise Vespa velutina, no if not.
community.submitSuccess: Photo submitted! It will appear after upload.
community.noSightings: No photo sightings yet. Be the first to submit one!

traps.title: My Traps
traps.subtitle: Register your traps once — then quickly log daily catches by access code or GPS proximity.
traps.name: Trap name
traps.logError: Failed to log catch. Please try again.
traps.notFound: Trap not found. Check the code and try again.
